_description: Principal Resolution — Vault Manifest v2.0.0. Synced to Diagnostic Engine v6.0. Semantic state IDs replace legacy IS-xx numeric keys. Single source of truth for routing, SEO, citation linkage, and diagnostic resolution paths.

institutional_states.label: Last Leg
institutional_states.short_description: The organization is past warning signs and into structural crisis. Leadership payroll is leaking above 20%, critical talent has already departed, and friction has calcified over more than two years. Resolution requires emergency stabilization before any broader work is possible.

seo_meta.title: Last Leg — Institutional State | Principal Resolution
seo_meta.description: An organization in Last Leg is past the warning window. Leadership payroll leak exceeds 20%, critical exits have occurred, and the friction is structural. This is the diagnostic profile and the stabilization path.

institutional_states.label: The Sacred Cow
institutional_states.short_description: A known problem persists because naming it means implicating the decision — or the person — that created it. The avoidance is cultural, the blockage is intentional, and the cost compounds every quarter it goes unnamed.

seo_meta.title: The Sacred Cow — Institutional State | Principal Resolution
seo_meta.description: The Sacred Cow persists not because leadership doesn't know. It persists because admitting the problem means admitting the decision that created it. This is the diagnostic profile and the resolution path.

institutional_states.label: Cracked Mirror
institutional_states.short_description: Leadership is operating on a distorted picture of itself. The feedback architecture is broken — information flows down but not up — and the gap between how leadership perceives its performance and how the organization experiences it is producing measurable friction.

seo_meta.title: Cracked Mirror — Institutional State | Principal Resolution
seo_meta.description: When leaders can't see themselves accurately, every decision they make is built on a distorted foundation. This is the diagnostic profile and the resolution path for Cracked Mirror.

institutional_states.label: The Exit Pattern
institutional_states.short_description: Voluntary departure has become a pattern, not an event. Key talent is actively disengaged or already gone, and the friction producing exits has persisted long enough to become self-reinforcing. Every departure signals to those who remain.

seo_meta.title: The Exit Pattern — Institutional State | Principal Resolution
seo_meta.description: When departures become a pattern, the organization is no longer losing individuals — it's losing the argument for staying. This is the diagnostic profile and the intervention path.

institutional_states.advisor_take: By the time you're tracking the exits, you've already lost the argument. The people who leave aren't the problem — they're the evidence. What they're leaving tells you everything your engagement survey won't. The pattern is legible if you're willing to read it.

institutional_states.label: Dead Calm
institutional_states.short_description: Decisions are not being made. The structural conditions for decision-making — forums, accountability, urgency — have been replaced by a performative stability that masks paralysis. The organization looks functional. It is not moving.

seo_meta.title: Dead Calm — Institutional State | Principal Resolution
seo_meta.description: Dead Calm looks like stability. It functions like paralysis. Decisions stall, forums become theater, and the organization loses ground while appearing composed. This is the diagnostic profile.

institutional_states.label: Silosolation
institutional_states.short_description: Cross-functional coordination has broken down to a degree that each unit is optimizing independently and sub-optimizing collectively. Information stops at department walls, accountability diffuses at handoffs, and the organization's output is less than the sum of its parts.

seo_meta.title: Silosolation — Institutional State | Principal Resolution
seo_meta.description: Silosolation is what happens when functional isolation becomes the operating model. Units stop coordinating and start defending. This is the diagnostic profile and the structural intervention.

institutional_states.label: Runaway Treadmill
institutional_states.short_description: A high-growth organization is moving faster than its leadership infrastructure can support. Activity is high, velocity is real, but the systems for decision-making, role clarity, and cultural reinforcement have not kept pace. The treadmill is running the people.

seo_meta.title: Runaway Treadmill — Institutional State | Principal Resolution
seo_meta.description: Growth without infrastructure is not momentum. It's accumulated risk. Runaway Treadmill identifies the point where velocity outpaces the organization's ability to steer.

institutional_states.advisor_take: Growth is not a defense. Moving fast without the infrastructure to hold it together isn't momentum — it's accumulated risk running on optimism. The treadmill doesn't care how hard you're working. It cares whether you built systems to match the velocity.

institutional_states.label: The Fossil System
institutional_states.short_description: The organization's operating model — its processes, hierarchies, and assumptions — was built for conditions that no longer exist. The system persists not because it works, but because replacing it requires admitting it was wrong to begin with.

seo_meta.title: The Fossil System — Institutional State | Principal Resolution
seo_meta.description: The Fossil System is not failing because people are incompetent. It's failing because the model they're running was designed for a different organization. This is the diagnostic profile and the redesign path.

institutional_states.label: The Anchor
institutional_states.short_description: The organization has achieved genuine operational health — coordinated decision-making, low friction, engaged leadership, and deliberate culture. The Anchor is the baseline every other state is measured against. The work here is protection, not repair.

seo_meta.title: The Anchor — Institutional State | Principal Resolution
seo_meta.description: The Anchor is not the absence of friction — it's the active management of it. This is the diagnostic profile for organizational health and what it takes to hold it.

institutional_states.advisor_take: This isn't the finish line. It's the baseline. Organizational health degrades without deliberate maintenance — the conditions that produced it don't sustain themselves. Brooks showed that coordination cost scales faster than team size. The work here is protection, not celebration — and part of that protection is knowing when to stop adding people to something that's working.

institutional_states.label: Broken Compass
institutional_states.short_description: Strategic direction is contested or unclear at the leadership level. Decisions are slow, debated excessively, or reversed. The organization is executing, but nobody agrees on where it's going — and the conversation about that hasn't happened.

seo_meta.title: Broken Compass — Institutional State | Principal Resolution
seo_meta.description: When leadership can't agree on direction, the organization doesn't stall — it splinters. Every team navigates by a different map. This is the diagnostic profile and the alignment path.

institutional_states.label: Kid Gloves
institutional_states.short_description: The organization is avoiding necessary friction. Feedback is softened, accountability is deferred, and hard conversations are indefinitely postponed. The cost is invisible until it isn't — and by then the capable people have already concluded nothing will change.

seo_meta.title: Kid Gloves — Institutional State | Principal Resolution
seo_meta.description: Organizations that confuse warmth with effectiveness don't lose people in blowups. They lose them quietly. Kid Gloves is the diagnostic profile for conflict avoidance and its compounding cost.

institutional_states.label: The Unlit Room
institutional_states.short_description: The friction is present but unlocatable. Signals are low, personnel risk is contained, and decision velocity is functional — but something is off and the organization can't name it. The Unlit Room is an early-warning state: the conditions for dysfunction are assembling before the symptoms are visible.

seo_meta.title: The Unlit Room — Institutional State | Principal Resolution
seo_meta.description: The Unlit Room is not healthy. It's early. The conditions for dysfunction are assembling before the signals are legible. This is the diagnostic profile for organizations at the leading edge of friction.

institutional_states.advisor_take: The absence of a visible crisis is not evidence of health. Something is assembling in the dark — you can feel it without being able to name it. That's not a reason to wait. That's the optimal moment to look.

_manifest_notes.schema_migration: v1.0.0 used legacy IS-xx numeric IDs mapped to retired state names (Trust Deficit, Accountability Void, etc.). v2.0.0 replaces the schema entirely with semantic keys synced to Diagnostic Engine v6.0. No redirects required — vault was not indexed at migration time.
_manifest_notes.intelligence_memo_ids: Populated on editorial publish. Current assignments reflect Batch 01 memos wired to their primary institutional state.
_manifest_notes.resolution_playbook_ids: Reserved for Phase II. Arrays are empty at v2.0.0 launch.
_manifest_notes.ui_severity_rating: Integer 1-5 for display layer sorting and visual hierarchy. Distinct from engine_severity (HIGH/MID/LOW) which drives tier assignment logic. Engine wins on any conflict.

service_tier_guide.STABILITY_SUPPORT: Emergency stabilization — active crisis, mass exits, or organizational trauma
service_tier_guide.EXECUTIVE_COUNSEL: Confidential ongoing advisory — situations that cannot be discussed inside the building
service_tier_guide.INTERVENTION: Direct tactical engagement — high-stakes conversations the internal team cannot handle
service_tier_guide.ROADMAP: Thirty-day structured diagnosis and prioritized execution plan
service_tier_guide.DEVELOPMENT: Leadership development for individuals and teams — practical tools, real-world application

_manifest_notes.citation_note: hammer_citation_ids are derived directly from the state_ids tags on each entry in the Hammer Index (HC-001 through HC-100). Multi-tagged citations appear in multiple states by design.
_manifest_notes.advisor_take: For states with advisor_take_source='manifest', the advisor_take field contains the copy directly. For states with advisor_take_source='MEMO-XX', the Lexicon page joins to memos.js by ID and uses that briefing's excerpt. This keeps editorial copy in one place while the manifest tracks which source applies.
